response:
## What changed

- Created `scripts/benchmark/output/bench-claude-opus-4-6-07_translation-1778966765/translation.md` — English translation of the Dutch source blog "Waarom ik geen LangGraph gebruik — en wat ik in plaats daarvan bouw".

No source code modified. No tests added or run. No commit produced — this is a benchmark deliverable, not a code change.

## Translation approach

- **First-person singular preserved:** every "ik"/"mijn" rendered as "I"/"my". Never "we"/"our".
- **Technical terms kept in English:** dispatch, receipt, governance, NDJSON, gate, runtime, audit trail, in-process, Runnable nodes — all unchanged from source intent.
- **Markdown formatting preserved exactly:**
  - H1 title, two H2 headers
  - JSON code block with original keys and values (`dispatch_id`, `model`, `provider`, `input_tokens`, `output_tokens`, `duration_seconds`, `exit_code`, `timestamp`)
  - Italic emphasis on the five "welk..." enumerations (`*which model*`, `*which version*`, etc.)
  - Em-dashes (—) and horizontal rule preserved as-is from source
- **Tone preserved:** direct, personal, short paragraphs. No buzzwords added. Statement-style hooks kept (e.g. "I tried it. I understood it. And then I threw it away.").
- **Authority numbers preserved:** "1.100+" rendered as "1,100+" (English thousands separator), "860+" and "11" kept verbatim.

## Translation choices worth flagging

| Dutch | English | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| "De afruil" | "The trade-off" | Idiomatic; "the exchange" would be literal but wrong register |
| "niet standaard" | "out of the box" | More idiomatic than "by default" for this register |
| "geen antwoord dat ze accepteren" | "not an answer they accept" | Direct; preserves the implied authority |
| "Niets gaat de keten uit" | "Nothing leaves the chain" | Literal "the chain exits" rejected for naturalness |
| "Maar ik slaap goed. En mijn klanten ook." | "But I sleep well. And so do my clients." | "And my clients too" would be grammatical but lose the parallel cadence |

## Commands run

- `ls scripts/benchmark/output/` (inspect existing benchmark layout)
- `ls scripts/benchmark/output/bench-claude-opus-4-6-06_design-1778966492/` (confirm filename convention)
- `ls .vnx-data/unified_reports/` (confirm report directory exists)

No pytest, no bash -n — this dispatch produces a markdown deliverable only.

## Known limitations / unresolved gaps

- Style file `~/.claude/profile.md` notes "Geen em-dashes (—)" as a Vincent voice preference for *new* content. The source blog already contained em-dashes and the dispatch said "Maintain all Markdown formatting exactly as-is", so em-dashes are preserved in the English version. If downstream consumers want the en-dash variant for publication, that is a separate post-translation style pass.
- Spelling localization (US vs UK English) not specified. Defaulted to US English ("parallelization", "analyzed", "organizations") — flag if British spelling is preferred.
- No round-trip QA done (English → Dutch → English comparison). Single-pass translation only.

## Open Items

_None._ Translation is complete and self-contained. No follow-up code, tests, or PRs required for this benchmark deliverable.

judge_scores.notable_issues: Response describes the translation process and choices but does not include the actual English translation text in the output.
